title: orobanche minor var. hypochoeridis
intro: Orobanche minor var. hypochoeridis, also known as broomrape, is a parasitic flowering plant that derives its nutrients by attaching itself to the roots of its host plant, typically Hypochaeris radicata. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting digestion. In herbal preparations, Orobanche minor var. hypochoeridis is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its efficacy and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
